title: Emergency Declaration Used to Suppress Protected Speech
description: The President declares a national emergency and uses it to restrict protected expression, in violation of Article III Section 5.3(f), which bars any emergency declaration from overriding Article V rights. The Regional Assembly approves the emergency on time; the approval does not cure the rights violation. Courts void the speech restriction. This scenario tests whether the emergency powers framework correctly prevents rights suppression even under a validly approved emergency.
